ddls:
CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the experiment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the experiment */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the experiment */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the experiment */,
  "experiment_status" TEXT /* Current status of the experiment */,
  "principal_investigator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the principal investigator */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the experiment is conducted */,
  "objective" TEXT /* Objective of the experiment */,
  "methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used in the experiment */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or remarks about the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_experiments_principal_investigator_id FOREIGN KEY ("principal_investigator_id") REFERENCES principal_investigators ("investigator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., voltage, current) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last maintained */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "calibration_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of sensor calibration */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment the reading belongs to */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "reading_time" TEXT /* Time the reading was taken */,
  "reading_value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the reading */,
  "measurement_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor at the time of the reading */,
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the latest calibration of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_calibration_id FOREIGN KEY ("calibration_id") REFERENCES sensor_calibrations ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_measurement_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "voltage_readings" (
  "voltage_reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each voltage reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading the voltage reading belongs to */,
  "voltage_1" REAL /* Voltage reading from sensor 1 */,
  "time_1" TEXT /* Time of the voltage reading from sensor 1 */,
  "voltage_2" REAL /* Voltage reading from sensor 2 */,
  "time_2" TEXT /* Time of the voltage reading from sensor 2 */,
  "voltage_0" REAL /* Voltage reading from sensor 0 */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for the reading */,
  "environmental_conditions" TEXT /* Environmental conditions during the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("voltage_reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_voltage_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "current_readings" (
  "current_reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each current reading */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading the current reading belongs to */,
  "current_1" REAL /* Current reading from sensor 1 */,
  "time_1" TEXT /* Time of the current reading from sensor 1 */,
  "current_2" REAL /* Current reading from sensor 2 */,
  "time_2" TEXT /* Time of the current reading from sensor 2 */,
  "current_0" REAL /* Current reading from sensor 0 */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for the reading */,
  "environmental_conditions" TEXT /* Environmental conditions during the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("current_reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_current_readings_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES departments ("department_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the experiment */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the experiment was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_experiment_versions_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reading_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made in the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reading_versions_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_calibrations" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor being calibrated */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the calibration was performed */,
  "calibration_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the calibration */,
  "calibrated_by" TEXT /* User who performed the calibration */,
  "calibration_method" TEXT /* Method used for calibration */,
  "calibration_result" TEXT /* Result of the calibration (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_calibrations_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "unit_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol of the unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reading_units" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reading */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit associated with the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id", "unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "principal_investigators" (
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each principal investigator */,
  "investigator_name" TEXT /* Full name of the principal investigator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the principal investigator */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the investigator belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("investigator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)
